title: Business Ethics

jokes.text: Not one of them called to congratulate me on our engagement... That, they might be off the hook for, because I didn't tell them.
jokes.setup: Pam is angry that her coworkers didn't congratulate her on her engagement
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals he never told them about the engagement
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with Jim's dry delivery undermining Pam's complaint. Character-specific humor that feels earned.

jokes.text: I thought you were already engaged. Nope. That was Roy. She was engaged to Roy. Thank you, Angela.
jokes.setup: Angela's confusion about Pam's engagement status
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic 'Thank you, Angela' for bringing up Pam's ex
jokes.explanation: Angela's bluntness creates awkwardness, Jim's sarcastic response lands well but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: I got a gift for Pam and Roy. Do I have to get another one?
jokes.setup: Learning about Pam's new engagement
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's practical concern about gift obligations
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Phyllis's practical, slightly selfish perspective. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: A little close to my engagement there, Tuna. What's your game here? To get married. She's not a virgin, you know.
jokes.setup: Andy confronting Jim about timing of engagement
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate and crude comment about Pam
jokes.explanation: Andy's inappropriate escalation creates cringe humor. Character-appropriate but relies on crude shock value.

jokes.text: Pencils down! Just kidding. Take your time.
jokes.setup: Holly giving stern instruction like a test proctor
jokes.punchline: Immediate backtrack showing her nervousness
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that establishes Holly's nervous, inexperienced character well.

jokes.text: It's going to be insane. No, it's not. I have to read from the binder.
jokes.setup: Holly hyping up the meeting as 'insane'
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation - she just has to read from a binder
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of expectations, shows Holly's self-aware insecurity about her presentation skills.

jokes.text: Let's get ethical, ethical / I want to get ethical / Let's get into ethics, yeah
jokes.setup: Michael trying to energize the ethics meeting
jokes.punchline: Singing 'Physical' by Olivia Newton-John but with 'ethical'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate song parody. Perfect character integration, though the wordplay is obvious.

jokes.text: And ultimately my strategy is to sort of merge this into a relationship without her even knowing.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's helping Holly
jokes.punchline: His creepy manipulation strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott cringe - his oblivious confession of manipulative romantic strategy to camera.

jokes.text: Come on. He's right there. He was hired.
jokes.setup: Holly saying someone was 'fired' without naming names
jokes.punchline: Kevin's confusion between 'fired' and 'hired' pointing at Ryan
jokes.explanation: Kevin's typical misunderstanding creates a nice wordplay moment that feels character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Check it out, hired guy. Nice.
jokes.setup: Following Kevin's 'hired' comment
jokes.punchline: Creed's casual acknowledgment of Kevin's mistake as fact
jokes.explanation: Creed's oblivious reinforcement of Kevin's mistake is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Even though it was an amazing ride and I'll give an example. Anyone see Survivor, Season Six? In New York City, I hooked up with a girl who looked exactly like that.
jokes.setup: Ryan supposedly showing regret for his misconduct
jokes.punchline: Bragging about hooking up with a Survivor contestant lookalike
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - turning his apology into a brag about his sexual conquests. Great character-driven cringe.

jokes.text: I thought 'very strongly agree' sounded stronger than 'totally agree.'
jokes.setup: Holly correcting the ethics quiz answers
jokes.punchline: Kevin's logical but wrong interpretation of the scale
jokes.explanation: Kevin's earnest logic about language intensity is endearing and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, I think we can all totally agree that Holly is totally fantastic.
jokes.setup: Discussion about 'totally agree' vs other options
jokes.punchline: Michael's obvious flirtation using 'totally' repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Michael's clumsy flirtation attempt. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This meeting.
jokes.setup: Holly asks for examples of time-wasting activities
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan response that the meeting itself is time-wasting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim - two words that brilliantly meta-comment on corporate meetings. Economy and structure are excellent.

jokes.text: Can't set them up like that.
jokes.setup: Jim's perfect comeback about the meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael's recognition that Holly gave Jim too easy a setup
jokes.explanation: Michael's awareness of comedy timing and setup/punchline structure is a nice meta moment.

jokes.text: I'll do it. I don't care. / I'll smoke with you. / I got a bag of cigars in my purse.
jokes.setup: Discussion about unfair smoking breaks
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation from Stanley's threat to smoke to Meredith having cigars ready
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation showing office solidarity. Meredith having cigars ready is a perfect character detail.

jokes.text: You need to be Robin Williams and M. Night Shyamalan. You need to be Robin Shyamalan.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining what makes meetings successful
jokes.punchline: Combining the names into 'Robin Shyamalan'
jokes.explanation: Creative name combination that's both absurd and reveals Michael's pseudo-intellectual approach to entertainment.

jokes.text: Lauren! Enough with the pencils!
jokes.setup: Holly using 'Lauren' as example name for stealing pencils
jokes.punchline: Michael's frustrated outburst as if Lauren is a real repeat offender
jokes.explanation: Michael treating a hypothetical person as real is perfectly in character and unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: I'll drop an ethics bomb on you. Would you steal bread to feed your family? Boom!
jokes.setup: Andy promising to deliver a profound ethical question
jokes.punchline: The cliché philosophy 101 bread-stealing dilemma with dramatic 'Boom!'
jokes.explanation: Andy's overconfident delivery of Philosophy 101 basics with dramatic flair is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It's a trick question. The bread is poisoned. Also, it's not your real family. You've been cuckolded by a stronger, smarter male.
jokes.setup: Andy's bread-stealing ethical dilemma
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd complications ending with 'cuckolded by a stronger, smarter male'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple ethics to absurd evolutionary psychology. The 'stronger, smarter male' is peak Andy insecurity.

jokes.text: I viewed Cookie Monster Sings Chocolate Rain about 1,000 times.
jokes.setup: Kevin confessing to workplace time theft
jokes.punchline: The specific, ridiculous YouTube video he watched repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin - the specific absurdity of combining Cookie Monster with 'Chocolate Rain' feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: What was the dilemma? To tell you or not.
jokes.setup: Holly asking about Kevin's ethical dilemma
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simple interpretation - the dilemma was whether to confess
jokes.explanation: Kevin's literal, simple take on ethics is endearing and character-consistent.

jokes.text: A siesta. Time thief. Time thief. Fire him!
jokes.setup: Stanley casually mentioning long lunches
jokes.punchline: Dwight's immediate, dramatic overreaction calling for firing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight overreaction to minor infractions. His authoritarian instincts are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You are a thief of joy.
jokes.setup: Dwight's rigid enforcement of rules
jokes.punchline: Oscar's poetic insult calling Dwight a 'thief of joy'
jokes.explanation: Beautiful phrase that perfectly captures Dwight's effect on others. Poetic and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I once reported Oscar to the INS. Turns out he's clean, but I'm glad I did it.
jokes.setup: Sharing ethical workplace confessions
jokes.punchline: Angela's xenophobic reporting of Oscar with no remorse
jokes.explanation: Perfectly reveals Angela's prejudiced character. Dark but character-appropriate humor.

jokes.text: Well, for the past six years I have been sleeping with him in exchange for discounts on our supplies and Outback Steakhouse gift certificates.
jokes.setup: Casual workplace ethics confessions
jokes.punchline: Meredith's bombshell revelation of prostitution for office supplies
jokes.explanation: Massive escalation that reframes the entire ethics seminar. Perfect Meredith - casual delivery of shocking behavior. Excellent structure and impact.

jokes.text: Jackpot.
jokes.setup: Meredith's prostitution revelation
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate excitement about the drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect one-word reaction showing Michael's misplaced priorities - excited about drama, not concerned about ethics.

jokes.text: Exchange of steak. Have you ever had sirloin steak, honey?
jokes.setup: Holly's concern about 'exchange of goods'
jokes.punchline: Meredith's casual attitude and condescending 'honey'
jokes.explanation: Meredith's complete lack of shame and condescending tone to the HR rep is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Meredith, 'the Merenator,' sleeping with suppliers.
jokes.setup: Meredith's revelation about sleeping with suppliers
jokes.punchline: Michael giving her an action movie nickname
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to give inappropriate nicknames in serious situations is character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Am I in trouble here or something? / Nothing unethical happened and that you just like to sleep around?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to help Meredith avoid consequences
jokes.punchline: His suggested defense makes her sound even worse
jokes.explanation: Michael's 'help' making things worse is classic. His logic that sleeping around randomly is better than prostitution is absurd.

jokes.text: There's not a lot of fruit in those looms. For the love of God, we're trying to help you, you stupid bag.
jokes.setup: Meredith being uncooperative with their help
jokes.punchline: Michael's frustrated insult calling her a 'stupid bag'
jokes.explanation: Michael's escalating frustration with someone he's trying to help is character-appropriate. 'Stupid bag' is a mildly creative insult.

jokes.text: Well, it's funny, maybe it's a girl thing, but after we did it, when he would give me those coupons, I just felt good about myself.
jokes.setup: Holly questioning why Meredith wanted the steak coupons
jokes.punchline: Meredith's touching but inappropriate explanation about feeling valued
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly touching moment that humanizes Meredith while remaining deeply inappropriate. Complex emotional comedy.

jokes.text: You had said that you don't do anything personal during work time, so I'm just making sure.
jokes.setup: Jim timing Dwight's yawn with a stopwatch
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan explanation for timing biological functions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim prank - taking Dwight's rigid standards to absurd logical conclusion. Perfect character dynamic.

jokes.text: Hey, look, monkey knows how to use a stop watch, everybody.
jokes.setup: Jim timing Dwight's personal activities
jokes.punchline: Dwight's frustrated insult calling Jim a monkey
jokes.explanation: Basic insult from frustrated Dwight. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: It's Mike-raculous. Reaching. You'll get there.
jokes.setup: Michael making a 'miraculous' pun with his name
jokes.punchline: His self-aware admission that it was a reach
jokes.explanation: Michael's awareness that his pun was bad but continuing anyway is perfectly in character. Meta humor works.

jokes.text: Would you care to bang it out over lunch? I already bought this.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting they work out the Meredith situation
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate phrasing of 'bang it out' while holding lunch
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael accidental innuendo. Character-appropriate but relies on obvious double entendre.

jokes.text: Terminate her. Terminator. 'I'm from the future.'
jokes.setup: Holly saying Meredith should be terminated
jokes.punchline: Michael's word association to Terminator movie
jokes.explanation: Basic word association humor. Michael's tendency to make movie references is in character but this is low-effort.

jokes.text: I mean, I like all the crazy monsters and stuff, you know, like Klingons and Wookiees and all that, but...
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to watch Battlestar Galactica
jokes.punchline: Confusing it with Star Wars and Star Trek elements
jokes.explanation: Andy's fake geek knowledge mixing up sci-fi franchises. Good setup for Dwight's reaction.

jokes.text: Really? That's cool. The story's kind of bland. It's about this guy named Dumbledore Calrissian who needs to return the ring back to Mordor.
jokes.setup: Jim pretending to agree about Battlestar Galactica being a remake
jokes.punchline: Completely mixing up Harry Potter, Star Wars, and Lord of the Rings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Andy's confusion. 'Dumbledore Calrissian' is inspired nonsense. Perfect Jim prank setup with excellent structure.

jokes.text: Really. That doesn't sound right.
jokes.setup: Jim's absurd plot description mixing multiple franchises
jokes.punchline: Dwight's massive understatement of 'doesn't sound right'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - his serious, understated reaction to obvious nonsense. Great straight-man delivery.

jokes.text: No, it's more of an underwear garment that has little spikes, like, made of... Like sometimes they're made of metal. You know what I'm talking about? You unlock a little door that... Down where you... Where you put... Where you put the...
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting a chastity belt to prevent Meredith's behavior
jokes.punchline: His increasingly uncomfortable and specific description
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael cringe - his knowledge of chastity belts and inability to articulate it appropriately creates sustained discomfort.

jokes.text: At 12:45, he sneezed while keeping his eyes open, which I always thought was impossible. At 1:32, he peed. And I know that because he did it in an open soda bottle under the desk while filling out expense reports.
jokes.setup: Jim timing Dwight's activities for ethical violations
jokes.punchline: Revealing Dwight's increasingly extreme work dedication including peeing in a bottle
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing Dwight's insane work ethic. The bottle detail is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: It's exhausting, being this vigilant. I'll probably have to go home early today.
jokes.setup: Jim's intense monitoring of Dwight
jokes.punchline: The irony that watching someone work is more tiring than working
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic conclusion to Jim's prank. The reversal that monitoring Dwight is harder than working is brilliantly executed.

jokes.text: And I am holding on to her leftovers.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's standing outside
jokes.punchline: The visual of him holding Holly's restaurant leftovers awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Nice visual detail showing Michael's awkward situation. The mundane detail of leftovers adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: So I guess I can assume that was personal.
jokes.setup: Jim and Holly's private conversation
jokes.punchline: Dwight continuing his time-monitoring obsession
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the time-monitoring subplot. Shows Dwight's persistence and rule obsession.

jokes.text: Turns out being the morality police does not make you popular. I should know, because in middle school I was the hall monitor and the kids used to stuff egg salad in my locker.
jokes.setup: Holly feeling isolated for enforcing ethics
jokes.punchline: Her specific, sad backstory about being unpopular as hall monitor
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Holly's history with rule enforcement. The egg salad detail is specific and pathetic.

jokes.text: What's the only thing worse than one HR rep? Two HR reps. You get me.
jokes.setup: Corporate HR calling while Holly is there
jokes.punchline: Michael's joke setup about two HR reps being worse
jokes.explanation: Basic setup/punchline structure. Michael's anti-HR sentiment is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I don't know, can you?
jokes.setup: Holly asking 'Can I sit?'
jokes.punchline: Michael's grammar teacher correction joke
jokes.explanation: Tired elementary school joke. Michael using it in a tense moment is character-appropriate but the joke itself is hacky.

jokes.text: Gently, with a rose?
jokes.setup: Michael wondering how to say 'I told you so'
jokes.punchline: His romantic suggestion involving a rose
jokes.explanation: Michael's romantic approach to criticism is absurd and character-specific. The rose detail is unexpectedly sweet.

jokes.text: Don't sign anything. / Get in there right now or I'm going to lose it!
jokes.setup: Holly trying to get signatures for the ethics training
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate contradiction and threat
jokes.explanation: Michael's quick flip from supporting employee rebellion to demanding compliance is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Wait a second, how does this steak factor in again? I think she got it as a tip, but I don't know why she didn't just take cash.
jokes.setup: Employees enjoying Meredith's steak dinner
jokes.punchline: Their casual discussion of her prostitution payment method
jokes.explanation: The casual, practical discussion of prostitution payment methods while eating the 'proceeds' is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I don't care what she's doing, I hope she just keeps doing it. Amen. Just keep the ribs coming.
jokes.setup: Employees benefiting from Meredith's unethical behavior
jokes.punchline: Their complete endorsement of her prostitution for office benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing the employees' complete moral flexibility when they benefit. Stanley's pragmatism is spot-on.

tmdb_overview: Holly leads a business ethics seminar as a result of Ryan's scandal, and when Michael encourages everyone to share their own unethical work behaviors, things get out of hand. Meanwhile, Jim messes with Dwight by requiring him to follow the Dunder Mifflin "Time Theft" policy.
